title: cacalia glabrata
intro: Cacalia glabrata, also known as the rough-hair American groundsel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family. Traditionally, Cacalia glabrata has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. Cacalia glabrata is often prepared in herbal teas or infusions, as well as tinctures, to utilize its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy.
